channel.title: development on Developer Blog
channel.description: Recent content in development on Developer Blog

item.title: Chapter I: Development
item.description: OOP Object-oriented programing is a way to develop software application using real-world terminologies to create entities that interact with one another using objects. 4 Componenet of object-oriented: Class: define the structure using methods and properties to describle real-world entity Methods: A method represents a particular behavior, this is action of real-world entity example Human class can have method: talk, speak, doTask… Properties: properties hold the data temporarily during the execution of an application

item.title: Chapter III:
item.description: Design Pattern In Software Development , We have many desgin pattern but you don’t know all. You only need one of design pattern in main category Createation Pattern: Builder Structural Pattern: Facade Behavior: Chain of Responsibility I will expain this in future. TODO: Add design pattern, and expain each group design pattern What, When, How.

item.title: Chapter II: Development
item.description: Hi, today I want to talk about SOLID in developement software this is the first lesson my boss teach me and tell me, if you want to go far in major software development. You have knowledge about SOLID and alway follow SOLID Principle. 1 - S: Single Responsibility Principle As the name of this principle, one class has only one responsibility and one reason to update code in a class is the client change business.

item.title: development
item.description: Xin chào m.n 👋 💬 Ask me about: https://fb.com/biccv [OOP] 1 - Basic OOP 2 - Abstraction 3 - Encapsulation 4 - Inheritance 5 - Polimorphism 6 - Method Overriding 7 - Method Hiding SOLID 1 - Single Responsibility Principle 2 - Open | Close Principle 3 - Liskov’s Subsitution Principle 4 - Interface Segregation Principle 5 - Dependency Inversion Principle 6 - you aren’t gonna need it 7 - Do one thing and do it right
